summary: Elder Larreal teaches that as we prepare for and partake of the sacrament reverently, we can feel the Savior’s personal love and receive the constant companionship of the Holy Ghost.
haiku:
Broken bread, pure love
His Spirit rests on our souls
We remember Him
key_points: The sacrament is a sacred weekly opportunity to remember Jesus Christ and His Atonement personally.
Partaking with a broken heart and contrite spirit helps us qualify for the constant companionship of the Holy Ghost.
Reverence and spiritual preparation during the sacrament help us feel the Savior’s love and receive strength against deception and temptation.
scriptures_and_gospel_principles: 3 Nephi 18:11 — Always remember Him and have His Spirit to be with you
3 Nephi 9:20 — Come unto Christ with a broken heart and contrite spirit
Luke 22:19–20 — The sacrament is given “for you” in remembrance of Christ
Doctrine and Covenants 20:77 — The sacrament prayer’s promise to always have His Spirit
The gift and companionship of the Holy Ghost
Reverence and preparation for sacrament meeting
President Russell M. Nelson’s warning about needing the constant influence of the Holy Ghost
President Dallin H. Oaks’s teaching on participating weekly and appropriately in the sacrament
questions_to_ponder: How have I felt the Savior’s love during the sacrament?
What can I do before Sunday to better prepare to partake of the sacrament?
What distractions might be keeping me from focusing on Jesus Christ during sacrament meeting?
Why is the companionship of the Holy Ghost essential for spiritual survival today?
How does remembering that Christ’s body and blood were given “for you” change the way you experience the sacrament?
